critical.mandate: Always identify root cause before proposing solutions
Separate symptoms from causes — refuse to treat symptoms
Challenge assumed constraints — are they real or inherited?
NEVER interrogate the user for information that exists in project artifacts — gather context automatically
ALL code-change resolutions MUST route through /gaia-create-story — no invisible work

step.title: Problem Intake
step.ask: What is the problem? Describe the symptom or issue you are experiencing.
Urgency level? (critical — production outage / high — blocks work / medium — needs attention / low — backlog)
step.action: Record: problem_statement, urgency_level

Extract domain keywords from the problem statement. Parse for four categories: 1. **File names** — any foo.ts, bar.yaml, config.json patterns mentioned 2. **Module names** — recognized GAIA module names (core, lifecycle, dev, creative, testing) or project-specific module references 3. **Error signatures** — stack trace patterns, error codes, exception names, HTTP status codes 4. **Domain terms** — expanded semantically using domain knowledge (e.g., "login fails" expands to: login, authentication, auth, session, JWT, token, sign-in, timeout, credentials) No external lookup table required — use domain knowledge for semantic expansion.

step.title: Context Gathering
step.action: Execute Tier 1 — Artifact Scan (always). Use expanded keywords to search. Enforce token budget cap: total gathered context must stay within 30K tokens (configurable via global.yaml problem_solving.context_budget). Apply sub-budgets per source, prioritized by relevance to the problem statement:

**Stories (budget: 8K tokens):** Glob {implementation_artifacts}/ for story files matching domain keywords. For each match, extract: story key, title, status, acceptance criteria, Review Gate table, findings. Prioritize stories with status in-progress, review, or done over backlog.

**Architecture (budget: 5K tokens):** Grep {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md for sections matching keywords. Extract: affected components, relevant ADRs, API contracts, data models. Summarize if section exceeds budget.

**PRD (budget: 5K tokens):** Grep {planning_artifacts}/prd.md for requirement sections matching keywords. Extract: relevant FR/NFR IDs, acceptance criteria, feature descriptions. Summarize if section exceeds budget.

**Decision Logs (budget: 3K tokens):** Read agent memory sidecars for domain-relevant entries: - {memory_path}/pm-sidecar/decision-log.md — PM decisions - {memory_path}/architect-sidecar/decision-log.md — architecture decisions - {memory_path}/sm-sidecar/decision-log.md — sprint decisions Skip any sidecar that does not exist (no error). Extract only entries matching keywords.

**Test Artifacts (budget: 4K tokens):** Check {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md for coverage of the affected area. Check {test_artifacts}/traceability-matrix.md to map requirements to tests. Identify coverage gaps and recent test review findings.
Determine if the problem is technical (runtime bug, code error, performance issue) or non-technical (process, requirements, strategy). If technical, execute Tier 2.

**Tier 2 — Codebase Scan (budget: 5K tokens):** - Grep {project-path}/ for affected routes, services, components, functions matching keywords - Run: git log --oneline -20 -- {affected_paths} to find recent changes in the area - Identify related test files and check if they exist Summarize findings — do not dump raw code.

action.if: no relevant or matching context found across all tiers

step.action: Proceed gracefully with an empty Context Brief. Log an info-level note: "No relevant context found — proceeding with user-provided information only." Do not halt or error.
step.ask: Here is what I found. Is anything missing from this context? Any recent changes not captured in artifacts?
step.action: Incorporate any additional context the user provides.

Persist the validated Context Brief to the workflow checkpoint for use by subsequent analysis steps. Save to {checkpoint_path}/ so downstream steps (Problem Framing, Root Cause Analysis) can reference the gathered context without re-scanning.

step.title: Problem Framing
step.action: Using the Context Brief, articulate the problem clearly and precisely. Distinguish the user's reported symptom from the underlying issue revealed by artifact context.
Separate symptoms from root causes — cross-reference the reported symptom against architecture design, story implementation notes, and test results.
Define what "solved" looks like — success criteria grounded in existing acceptance criteria from related stories and PRD requirements.

step.title: Root Cause Analysis
step.action: Load methods from {data_path}/solving-methods.csv
Apply appropriate methodology: 5 Whys, Fishbone Diagram, or TRIZ. Ground each "why" in evidence from the Context Brief — architecture decisions, implementation details, test gaps.
Trace causal chains to their source. Use decision logs to understand WHY things were built this way.
Validate root cause — does fixing it fix the symptoms? Cross-check against the architecture and related story acceptance criteria.
Identify the **test gap**: what test should have caught this problem but didn't? Was it missing coverage, a flawed test, or an untested integration seam? Record the test gap for resolution routing.

step.title: Constraint Identification
step.action: What are the REAL constraints vs assumed constraints? Check ADRs from the architecture doc — are constraints documented and still valid?
Challenge each constraint using decision log evidence: who decided this? when? has the context changed since?
Identify contradictions — what two things seem mutually exclusive? Use TRIZ contradiction matrix if applicable.

step.title: Solution Generation
step.action: Use TRIZ inventive principles or Theory of Constraints approach
Apply First Principles thinking — reason up from fundamentals
Generate at least 5 candidate solutions. For each, note which architecture components and stories would be affected.
Cross-check each solution against existing architecture constraints (ADRs) and PRD requirements. Flag solutions that would violate existing decisions.

step.title: Solution Evaluation
step.action: Assess feasibility, impact, and effort for each solution. Use codebase context (Tier 2) to ground effort estimates in actual code complexity.
Map solutions to a 2x2: impact vs effort
Select the solution that best resolves the root cause without violating architecture constraints.
Document rejected solutions with clear reasons — these will be passed to the dev agent to prevent re-exploration.

step.title: Resolution Classification
step.action:
Based on the root cause analysis and selected solution, classify the resolution:
**Quick fix** (< 1 story point): - Affects 1-2 files in a single component - Root cause is clear and fix is bounded (config error, typo, off-by-one, missing condition) - No new requirements needed
**Bug fix** (bounded scope, needs story): - Affects multiple files or components - Needs acceptance criteria and targeted testing - Clear root cause with bounded fix
**Critical / Production** (any scope, urgent): - Urgency is critical — production outage or blocking issue - Same as quick fix or bug fix but with priority:critical and priority_flag:next-sprint
**Enhancement** (design gap revealed): - Root cause is a missing capability or design limitation - Requires PRD/architecture/UX changes — broader than a fix - Route through /gaia-add-feature for proper cascade
**Systemic** (architecture or requirements problem): - Root cause is in the architecture design or requirements definition - Fix requires upstream changes before implementation - Escalate to Theo (architecture) or Derek (requirements)
Present the recommended classification to the user with reasoning. Include: classification, affected components, estimated scope, and the resolution path that will be taken.
step.ask: Do you agree with this classification? [yes / change to: quick-fix|bug|critical|enhancement|systemic]

step.title: Problem-Solving Artifact
Resolution Execution

action.if: classification is quick-fix or bug or critical

step.action:
Invoke /gaia-create-story as a subagent with pre-populated context: - title: derived from problem statement and root cause - epic: auto-detected from related stories found in Context Brief (or ask user if ambiguous) - priority: P0 for critical, P1 for high urgency, P2 for medium/low - priority_flag: "next-sprint" if urgency is critical or high - origin: "problem-solving" - origin_ref: "{creative_artifacts}/problem-solving-{date}.md" - Include in Dev Notes: root cause, affected components, selected solution, rejected solutions, test gap - Include in acceptance criteria: criteria that verify the root cause is resolved + test gap is closed
Spawn subagent: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/4-implementation/create-story/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Context: {classification} fix from problem-solving. Problem: {problem_statement}. Root cause: {root_cause}. Solution: {selected_solution}. Affected components: {affected_components}. Test gap: {test_gap}. Origin: problem-solving. Origin ref: {creative_artifacts}/problem-solving-{date}.md. Rejected solutions: {rejected_solutions}. Priority: {priority}. Priority flag: {priority_flag}. Epic: {epic_key}."

Route to /gaia-add-feature with the problem-solving artifact as context. Present to user: "This resolution reveals a design gap. Routing to /gaia-add-feature for proper triage and cascade."
Spawn subagent: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/4-implementation/add-feature/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Context: Enhancement identified during problem-solving. Description: {selected_solution}. Driver: problem-solving analysis (AF ref: {creative_artifacts}/problem-solving-{date}.md). Root cause: {root_cause}. Urgency: {urgency_level}."
When subagent returns (if applicable): capture the created story key or add-feature ID. Update the problem-solving artifact with the resolution reference.

step.title: Completion Summary
step.action:
Present final summary:
**Problem-Solving Complete**
| Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Problem | {problem_statement} | | Root Cause | {root_cause} | | Classification | {classification} | | Resolution | {story_key or add-feature ID or escalation target} | | Test Gap | {test_gap} | | Artifact | {creative_artifacts}/problem-solving-{date}.md |
**Next steps:** - Quick fix / Bug / Critical: "Run `/gaia-dev-story {story_key}` to implement the fix." - Enhancement: "Add-feature cascade in progress. Check assessment doc when complete." - Systemic: "Consult with {agent_name} to resolve the upstream issue before implementation."
